uses: Uva narum is native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia, and its medicinal uses have been documented in traditional medicine. Uva narum has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis and eczema. intro:
There is no information on the plant Uva narum. However, I found that Uva ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) is a type of plant that is also referred to as Uva narum by some sources.

Uva ursi, also known as Uva narum, is a small evergreen shrub native to the cooler regions of the Northern Hemisphere, whose leaves and fruits have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.

The health benefits of Uva ursi include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which have been used to treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Uva ursi include arbutin, hydroquinone, and methylhydroquinone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Uva ursi is often used in traditional medicine to treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other conditions. The most common herbal preparations made with Uva ursi include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
